claim_boundary: TICKET117 proves exact finite signed-dyadic endpoint and Gram identities and audits an independent-block contract. It does not prove the required uniform bilinear estimate, any of the four conjectures, or a conjecture counterexample.

exact_dyadic_lift.time_identity: II(n)=sum_B II_B(n), where B runs over the actual truncated dyadic outer-divisor shells and II_B retains mu(d) inside the block.
exact_dyadic_lift.endpoint_identity: P_{q,a}=sum_B P_{q,a}^{(B)} by linearity of the DFT, minor-cell sum, and Farey endpoint map.
exact_dyadic_lift.mean_identity: M_q=sum_B M_q^{(B)} before any absolute value is taken.
exact_dyadic_lift.centered_identity: Z_q=sum_B Z_q^{(B)} after complex centering in the same numerator space.

gram_identity.meaning: The real Gram matrix retains every within-block norm and cross-block cancellation or reinforcement exactly.
gram_identity.scope: This is an exact finite-dimensional identity, not a denominator-summed asymptotic dispersion estimate.

independent_block_triangle_audit.tested_contract: Pay sum_B |Re(M_q^{(B)}H_q)| and sum_B ||Z_q^{(B)}||_2 ||rho_q-mean rho_q||_2 for every q.
independent_block_triangle_audit.deterministic_order: The independent-block budget is at least the fully signed budget by scalar and vector triangle inequalities.
independent_block_triangle_audit.finite_result: Singleton dyadic blocks worsen the fully signed budget on every row and close none. Adjacent width-two grouping recovers most of the loss and is only 1,236.3 above finite closure at 4M, but still closes no audited row.

adjacent_pair_frontier.restriction: Use one fixed contiguous partition per horizon and place at most two adjacent dyadic shells in each group; never optimize by denominator.
adjacent_pair_frontier.stability_observation: The same adjacent-pair pattern is selected at 2M and 4M. This is finite evidence only and is not promoted to an eventual-stability theorem.

finite_frontier.interpretation: All values are finite measured transforms and do not establish a uniform-in-X theorem.

retained_asymptotic_criterion.statement: Prove a denominator-summed signed adjacent-dyadic-pair endpoint estimate that controls scalar means and within-pair Gram interactions with a fixed all-sufficiently-large-X margin.
retained_asymptotic_criterion.positive_scale_obligation: Prove positivity of the same major-plus-Type-I comparison scale on the identical X range.
retained_asymptotic_criterion.counterexample_route: Construct an unbounded sequence of Vaughan-realizable dyadic endpoint Gram matrices whose independent-block inflation or adverse off-diagonal energy defeats every fixed subcritical margin.

discarded_routes: Replace signed dyadic blocks by separate mu-positive and mu-negative layers.
Delete the off-diagonal Gram entries before testing their sign and magnitude.
Infer a uniform cross-block covariance sign from finitely many horizons.
Treat finite closure as a Twin Prime proof.

literature_boundary.established_background: Dyadic Type-II decomposition, bilinear estimates, and averaged shifted-Mobius cancellation are established tools.
literature_boundary.project_specific_result: The exact signed dyadic lift through this endpoint map and its finite real-Gram ledger are project-specific.
literature_boundary.non_transfer: The cited works do not supply the fixed-shift, denominator-summed endpoint Gram estimate required here.

twin_dyadic_mobius_endpoint_gram_audit.proof_boundary: TICKET117 proves exact finite signed-dyadic endpoint and Gram identities and audits an independent-block contract. It does not prove the required uniform bilinear estimate, any of the four conjectures, or a conjecture counterexample.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: preserve the independent target during the Twin dyadic-Gram audit
attempts.attempt: Preserve the existing infinite target; no Twin endpoint-Gram shortcut is transferred.
attempts.obstruction: TICKET117 supplies no new target-specific infinite theorem for this problem.
attempts.next_experiment: Continue NonCircularExplicitFormulaKernelPositivity with its own proof and counterexample oracle.
attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: exact signed dyadic lift, real-Gram ledger, and independent-block audit
attempts.attempt: Keep mu(d) inside actual dyadic outer-divisor blocks, map every block to the same Farey endpoints, and retain all cross-block Gram entries before testing a blockwise bound.
attempts.obstruction: The measured block cancellation remains finite data; no uniform denominator-summed signed bilinear estimate controls the full endpoint Gram contribution yet.
attempts.next_experiment: Localize the adverse Gram mass by dyadic block pair and denominator, then prove a large-sieve/dispersion bound for the retained signed pairs or construct an unbounded Vaughan-realizable margin failure.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no certified last-twin counterexample.
